author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Of the words of St. Matthew’s Gospel, Chap. iii. 13, 'Then Jesus cometh from Galilee to the Jordan unto John, to be baptized of him.' Concerning the Trinity.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Of the words of St. Matthew’s Gospel, Chap. iii. 13, 'Then Jesus cometh from Galilee to the Jordan unto John, to be baptized of him.' Concerning the Trinity.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Gospel hath set before me a subject whereof to speak to you, beloved, as though by the Lord’s command, and by His command in very deed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For my heart hath waited for an order as it were from Him to speak, that I might understand thereby that it is His wish that I should speak on that which He hath also willed should be read to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let your zeal and devotion then give ear, and before the Lord our God Himself aid ye my labour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we behold and see as it were in a divine spectacle exhibited to us, the notice of our God in Trinity, conveyed to us at the river Jordan.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when Jesus came and was baptized by John, the Lord by His servant (and this He did for an example of humility; for He showeth that in this same humility is righteousness fulfilled, when as John said to Him, “I have need to be baptized of Thee, and comest Thou to me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered, “Suffer it to be so now, that all righteousness may be fulfilled”), when He was baptized then, the heavens were opened, and the Holy Spirit came down upon Him in the form of a Dove: and then a Voice from on high followed, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then we have the Trinity in a certain sort distinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father in the Voice,—the Son in the Man,—the Holy Spirit in the Dove.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was only needful just to mention this, for most obvious is it to see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the notice of the Trinity is here conveyed to us plainly and without leaving room for doubt or hesitation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Christ Himself coming in the form of a servant to John, is doubtlessly the Son: for it cannot be said that it was the Father, or the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Jesus,” it is said, “cometh;” that is, the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who hath any doubt about the Dove? or who saith, “What is the Dove?” when the Gospel itself most plainly testifieth, “The Holy Spirit descended upon Him in the form of a dove.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in like manner as to that voice there can be no doubt that it is the Father’s, when He saith, “Thou art My Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus then we have the Trinity distinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if we consider the places, I say with confidence (though in fear I say it), that the Trinity is in a manner separable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Jesus came to the river, He came from one place to another; and the Dove descended from heaven to earth, from one place to another; and the very Voice of the Father sounded neither from the earth, nor from the water, but from heaven; these three are as it were separated in places, in offices, and in works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But one may say to me, “Show the Trinity to be inseparable rather.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember that thou who art speaking art a Catholic, and to Catholics art thou speaking.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus doth our faith teach, that is, the true, the right Catholic faith, gathered not by the opinion of private judgment, but by the witness of the Scriptures, not subject to the fluctuations of heretical rashness, but grounded on Apostolic truth: this we know, this we believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This though we see it not with our eyes, nor as yet with the heart, so long as we are being purified by faith, yet by this faith we most lightly and most strenuously maintain—That the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are a Trinity inseparable; One God, not three Gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet so One God, as that the Son is not the Father, and the Father is not the Son, and the Holy Spirit is neither the Father nor the Son, but the Spirit of the Father and of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This ineffable Divinity, abiding ever in itself, making all things new, creating, creating anew, sending, recalling, judging, delivering, this Trinity, I say, we know to be at once ineffable and inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What am I then about?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See: The Son came separately in the Man; The Holy Spirit descended separately from heaven in the form of a Dove; The Voice of the Father sounded separately out of heaven, “This is My Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is this inseparable Trinity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath made you attentive by my words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pray for me, and open, as it were, the folds of your hearts, and may He grant you wherewith your hearts so opened may be filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Share my travail with me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For you see what I have undertaken; and not only what, but who I am that have undertaken it, and of what I wish to speak, and where and what my position is, even in that “body which is corruptible, and presseth down the soul, and the earthly habitation weigheth down the mind that museth upon many things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When therefore I abstract my mind from the multiplicity of things, and gather it up into the One God, the inseparable Trinity, that so I may see something which I may say of it, think ye that in this “body which presseth down the soul,” I shall be able to say (in order that I may speak to you something worthy of the subject), “O Lord, I have lifted up my soul unto Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May He assist me, may He lift it up with me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I am too infirm in respect of Him, and He in respect of me is too mighty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this is a question which is often proposed by the most earnest brethren, and often has place in the conversation of the lovers of God’s word; for this much knocking is wont to be made unto God, while men say, “Doeth the Father anything which the Son doeth not? or doeth the Son anything which the Father doeth not?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us first speak of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when He to Whom we say, “Be Thou my helper, leave me not,” shall have given good success to this essay of ours, then shall we understand how that the Holy Spirit also is in no way separated from the operation of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As concerning the Father and the Son, then, brethren, give ear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doeth the Father anything without the Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We answer, No. Do you doubt it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what doeth He without Him “by Whom all things were made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things,” saith the Scripture, “were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to inculcate it fully upon the slow, and hard, and disputatious it added, And without Him was not anything made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then, brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We understand then by this that the whole creation which was made by the Son, the Father made by His Word—God, by His Power and Wisdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall we then say, “All things” indeed when they were created, “were made by Him,” but now the Father doeth not all things by Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be such a thought as this far from the hearts of believers; be it driven away from the mind of the devout; from the understanding of the godly!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It cannot be that He created by Him, and doth not govern by Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid that what existeth should be governed without Him, when by Him it was made, that it might have existence!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let us show by the testimony of the same Scripture that not only were all things created and made by Him as we have quoted from the Gospel, “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made,” but that the things which were made are also governed and ordered by Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You acknowledge Christ then to be the Power and Wisdom of God; acknowledge too what is said of Wisdom, “She reacheth from one end to another mightily, and sweetly doth she order all things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not then doubt that by Him are all things ruled, by whom all things were made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Father doeth nothing without the Son, nor the Son without the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But so a difficulty meets us, which we have undertaken to solve in the Name of the Lord, and by His will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Father doeth nothing without the Son, nor the Son without the Father, will it not follow, that we must say that the Father also was born of the Virgin Mary, the Father suffered under Pontius Pilate, the Father rose again and ascended into heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We do not say this, because we do not believe it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For I believed, therefore have I spoken: we also believe, and therefore speak.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is in the Creed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the Son was born of a Virgin, not the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is in the Creed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the Son suffered under Pontius Pilate and was dead, not the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have we forgotten, that some, misunderstanding this, are called “Patripassians,” who say that the Father Himself was born of a woman, that the Father Himself suffered, that the Father is the same as the Son, that they are two names, not two things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And these hath the Church Catholic separated from the communion of saints, that they might not deceive any, but dispute in separation from her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then recall the difficulty of the question to your minds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One may say to me, “You have said that the Father doeth nothing without the Son, nor the Son without the Father, and testimonies you have adduced out of the Scriptures, that the Father doeth nothing without the Son, for that ‘all things were made by Him;’ and again, that that which was made is not governed without the Son, for that He is the Wisdom of the Father, ‘reaching from one end to another mightily, and sweetly ordering all things.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now you tell me, as if contradicting yourself, that the Son was born of a Virgin, and not the Father; the Son suffered, not the Father; the Son rose again, not the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, here I see the Son doing something which the Father doeth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do you therefore either confess that the Son doeth something without the Father, or else that the Father also was born and suffered, and died and rose again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say one or the other of these, choose one of the two.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No: I will choose neither, I will say neither the one nor the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will neither say the Son doeth anything without the Father, for I should lie were I to say so; nor that the Father was born, suffered, and died, and rose again, for I should equally lie were I to say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How then, saith he, will you disentangle yourself from these straits?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The proposing of the question pleases you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May God grant His aid, that its solution may please you too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, what I am asking Him, that He would free both me and you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in one faith do we stand in the Name of Christ; and in one house do we live under one Lord, and in one body are we members under One Head, and by One Spirit are we quickened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the Lord then may set both me who speak, and you who hear, free from the straits of this most perplexing question, I say as follows: The Son indeed and not the Father was born of the Virgin Mary; but this very birth of the Son, not of the Father, was the work both of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father indeed suffered not, but the Son, yet the suffering of the Son was the work of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father did not rise again, but the Son, yet the resurrection of the Son was the work of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We seem then to be already quit of this question, but peradventure it is only by words of my own; let us see whether it is not as well by words divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is my place then to prove by testimonies of the sacred books, that the birth, and passion, and resurrection of the Son were in such sort the works of the Father and the Son, that whereas it is the birth, and passion, and resurrection of the Son only, yet these three things which belong to the Son only, were wrought neither by the Father alone, nor by the Son alone, but by the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us prove each several point, you hear as judges; the case has been already laid open; now let the witnesses come forth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let your judgment say to me, as is wont to be said to pleaders in a cause, “Establish what you promise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will do so assuredly, with the Lord’s assistance, and will cite the books of heavenly law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have listened to me attentively while proposing the question, listen now with still more attention while I prove my point.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I must first teach you concerning the birth of Christ, how it is the work of the Father and the Son, though what the Father and the Son did work pertains only to the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will quote Paul; one competently versed in the divine law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Paul, I say, will I quote, who prescribes the laws of peace, not of litigation, for lawyers at this day also have a Paul who prescribes the laws of the courts, not the Christian’s laws.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the holy Apostle show us then how the birth of the Son was the work of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But,” saith he, “when the fulness of time was come, God sent forth His Son, made of a woman, made under the Law, to redeem them that were under the Law.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus have ye heard him, and because it is plain and express, have understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, the Father made the Son to be born of a Virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “when the fulness of time was come, God sent His Son;” the Father sent His Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How sent He Him? “made of a woman, made under the Law.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father then made Him of a woman under the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth this peradventure perplex you, that I said of a virgin, and Paul saith of a woman?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not this perplex you; let us not stop here, for I am not speaking to persons without instruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scripture saith both, both “of a virgin,” and “of a woman.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where saith it, “of a virgin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “of a woman,” as you have just heard; here there is no contradiction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the peculiarity of the Hebrew tongue gives the name of “women” not to such as have lost their virgin estate, but to females generally.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have a plain passage in Genesis, when Eve herself was first made, “He made her a woman.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Scripture also in another place saith, that God ordered “the women” to be separated “which had not known man by lying with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then ought now to be well established, and should not detain us, that so we may be able to explain, by the Lord’s assistance, what will deservedly detain us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have then proved that the birth of the Son was the work of the Father; now let us prove that it was the work of the Son also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what is the birth of the Son of the Virgin Mary?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely it is His assumption of the form of a servant in the Virgin’s womb.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is the birth of the Son ought else, but the taking of the form of a servant in the womb of the Virgin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now hear how that this was the work of the Son also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who when He was in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God, but emptied Himself, taking upon Him the form of a servant.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When the fulness of time was come, God sent forth His Son, made of a woman,” who was “made His Son of the seed of David according to the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this then we see that the birth of the Son was the work of the Father; but in that the Son Himself “emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant,” we see that the birth of the Son was the work also of the Son Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then has been proved; so let us pass on from this point, and receive ye with attention that which comes next in order.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us prove that the Passion also of the Son was the work of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We may see that the Passion of the Son is the work of the Father, since it is written, “Who spared not His own Son, but delivered Him up for us all;” and that the Passion of the Son was His own work also, “Who loved me, and gave Himself for me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father delivered up the Son, and the Son delivered up Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Passion was wrought out for one, but by both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As therefore the birth, so the Passion, of Christ, was not the work of the Son without the Father, nor of the Father without the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father delivered up the Son, and the Son delivered up Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did Judas in it, but his own sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then pass on from this point also, and come we to the resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see the Son indeed, and not the Father, rising again, but both the Father and the Son working the resurrection of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The resurrection of the Son is the work of the Father; for it is written, “Wherefore He exalted Him, and gave Him a name which is above every name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father therefore raised the Son to life again, in exalting, and awakening Him from the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And did the Son also raise Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly He did.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He said of the temple, as the figure of His own body, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lastly, as the laying down of life has reference to the Passion, so the taking it again has reference to the resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see then if the Son laid down His life indeed, and the Father restored His life to Him, and not He to Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that the Father restored it is plain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so saith the Psalm, “Raise Thou Me up, and I will requite them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why do ye wait for a proof from me that the Son also restored life to Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Him speak Himself; “I have power to lay down My life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have not yet said what I promised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have said, “to lay it down;” and you are crying out already, for you are flying past me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For well-instructed as ye are in the school of your heavenly teacher, as attentively listening to, and in pious affection rehearsing, what is read, ye are not ignorant of what comes next.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have power,” saith He,“to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No man taketh it from Me, but I lay it down of Myself, and take it again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have made good what I promised; I have established my propositions with, as I think, the strongest proofs and testimonies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hold fast then what you have heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will recapitulate it briefly, and entrust it to be stored up in your minds as a thing, to my thinking, of the greatest usefulness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father was not born of the Virgin; yet this birth of the Son from the Virgin was the work both of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father suffered not on the Cross; yet the Passion of the Son was the work both of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father rose not again from the dead; yet the resurrection of the Son was the work both of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You see then a distinction of Persons, and an inseparableness of operation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not say therefore that the Father doeth any thing without the Son, or the Son any thing without the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps you have a difficulty as to the miracles which Jesus did, lest peradventure He did some which the Father did not!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is that saying, “The Father who dwelleth in Me, He doeth the works?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All that I have now said was plain; it needed to be barely mentioned; there was no necessity for much labour to make it understood, but only that care should be taken, that it might be brought to your remembrance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I wish to say something further, and here ask sincerely both for your more earnest attention, and your devotion to Godward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For none but bodies are held or contained in places suited to the nature of bodies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Divinity is beyond all such places: let no one seek for it as though it were in space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is everywhere invisible and inseparably present; not in one part greater, and another smaller; but whole everywhere, and nowhere divided.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can comprehend this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us restrain ourselves: let us remember who we are; and of Whom we speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let this and that, or whatever appertains to the nature of God, be with a pious faith embraced, with a holy respect entertained, and as far as is allowed us, as far as is possible for us, in an unspeakable sort understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let words be hushed: let the tongue be silent, let the heart be aroused, let the heart be lifted up thither.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not of such a nature as that it can ascend into the heart of man; but the heart of man must itself ascend to it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider the creatures (“for the invisible things of Him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made”), if haply in the things which God hath made, with which we have some familiarity of intercourse, we may find some resemblance, whereby we may prove that there are some three things which may be exhibited as three separably, yet whose operation is inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come, brethren, give me your whole attention.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But first of all consider what it is that I promise; if haply I can find any resemblance in the creature, for the Creator is too high above us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And peradventure some one of us, whose mind the glare of truth hath, as it were, stricken with sparks of its brightness, can say those words, “I said in my ecstasy.”—What saidst thou in thine ecstasy?—“I am cast away from the sight of Thine eyes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it seems to me as if he who said this had lifted up his soul unto God, and had been carried beyond himself, while they said daily unto him, “Where is thy God?”—had reached by a kind of spiritual contact to that unchangeable Light, and through the weakness of his sight had been unable to endure it, and so had fallen back again into his own, as it were, sick and languid state, and had compared himself with that Light, and had felt that the eye of his mind could not yet be attempered to the light of God’s wisdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because he had done this in ecstasy, hurried away from his bodily senses, and taken up into God, when he was recalled in a manner from God to man, he said, “I said in my ecstasy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I saw in ecstasy I know not what, which I could not long endure, and being restored to my mortal estate, and the manifold thoughts of mortal things from the body which presseth down the soul, I said, what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am cast away from the sight of Thine eyes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art far above, and I am far below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then, brethren, shall we say of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou hast been able to comprehend what thou wouldest say, it is not God; if thou hast been able to comprehend it, thou hast comprehended something else instead of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hast been able to comprehend Him as thou thinkest, by so thinking thou hast deceived thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then is not God, if thou hast comprehended it; but if it be God, thou hast not comprehended it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How therefore wouldest thou speak of that which thou canst not comprehend?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see then, if haply we cannot find something in the creature whereby we may prove that some three things are exhibited separately whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whither shall we go?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the heaven, to dispute of the sun and moon and stars?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the earth, to dispute of shrubs, and trees, and animals which fill the earth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or of the heaven and the earth itself, which contain all the things that are in heaven and earth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How long, O man, wilt thou roam over the creation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Return unto thyself, see, consider, examine thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art searching among the creatures for some three things which are separately exhibited, whose operation is yet inseparable; if then thou art searching for this among the creatures, search for it first in thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou art not other than a creature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a resemblance thou art searching for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou search for it among the cattle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of God it was thou wast speaking, when thou wast in search for this resemblance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast speaking of the Trinity of Majesty ineffable, and because thou didst fail in contemplating the Divine Nature, and with becoming humility didst confess thine infirmity, thou didst come down to human nature; there then pursue thine enquiry.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wilt thou make thy search among the cattle, in the sun, or the stars?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What of these was made after the image and likeness of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou mayest search in thine own self for something more familiar to thee, and more excellent than all these.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God made man after His own image and likeness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Search then in thine own self, if haply the image of the Trinity bear not some vestige of the Trinity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is this image?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is an image very different from its model; yet different as it is, it is an image and a likeness notwithstanding, not indeed in the same way as the Son is the Image, being the Same Which the Father is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For an image is in one sort in a son, and in another in a mirror.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is great difference between them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thine image in thy son is thine own self, for the son is by nature what thou art.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In substance the same as thou, in person other than thou.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man then is not an image as the Only-begotten Son is, but made after a sort of image and likeness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him then search for something in himself, if so be he may find it, even for some three things which are exhibited separately, whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will search, and do ye search with me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will not search in you, but do ye search in yourselves, and I in myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us search in concert, and in concert discuss our common nature and substance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, O man, and consider whether what I am saying be true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou a body and flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have, you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how am I in this place that I now occupy, and how do I move from place to place?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How do I hear the words of one who is speaking, but by the ears of my body?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How do I see the mouth of him who is speaking, but by the eyes of my body?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is plain then that thou hast a body, no need is there to trouble one’s self about so plain a matter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider then another point, consider what it is that acts through this body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hearest by means of the ear, but it is not the ear that hears.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is something else within which hears by means of the ear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seest by means of the eye—examine this eye.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! hast thou acknowledged the house, and paid no regard to him that inhabiteth it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth the eye see by itself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it not another that sees by means of the eye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will not say, that the eye of a dead man, from whose body it is plain the inhabitant hath departed, sees not, but any man’s eye who is only thinking of something else, sees not the form of the object that is before him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look then into thine inner man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there it is rather that the resemblance must be sought for of some three things which are exhibited separately, whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is in thy mind?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure if I search, I find many things there, but there is something very nigh at hand, which is understood more easily.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is in thy soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Call it to mind, reflect upon it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I do not require that credit should be given me in what I am about to say; if thou find it not in thyself, admit it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look inward then; but first let us see what had escaped me, whether man be not the image, not of the Son only, or of the Father only, but of the Father and the Son, and so consequently of course of the Holy Ghost also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The words in Genesis are, “Let Us make man after Our own image and likeness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Father doth not act without the Son, nor the Son without the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let Us make man after Our own image and likeness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us make,” not, “I will make,” or “Make thou,” or “Let him make,” but, “Let Us make after,” not “thine image,” or “mine,” but, “after Our image.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am asking, I am speaking remember of a distant resemblance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So let no one say, See what he has compared to God!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have advertised you of this already, and by anticipation have both put you on your guard, and have guarded myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The two are indeed very far removed from each other, as the lowest from the Highest, as the changeable from the Unchangeable, the created from the Creator, the human nature from the Divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I apprise you of this at first, that no one may say ought against me, because there is so great a difference in the things whereof I am about to speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lest then while I am asking for your ears, ye should any of you be getting ready your teeth, remember I have undertaken merely to show, that there are some three things which are separately exhibited, whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How like or how unlike these things are to the Almighty Trinity is no concern of mine at present; but in the very creatures of the lowest order, and subject to change, we do find three things which may be separately exhibited, whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O carnal imagination! obstinate, unbelieving conscience!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why as concerning that ineffable Majesty dost thou doubt as to that thing, which thou canst discover in thine own self?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I ask thee, O man, hast thou memory?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If not, how hast thou retained what I have said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps thou hast forgotten already what I said but a little while ago.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet these very words, “I said”—these two syllables, thou couldest not retain except by memory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how shouldest thou know they were two, if as the second sounded, thou hadst forgotten the first?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why do I dwell longer on this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why am I so urgent?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do I so press conviction?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hast memory; it is plain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am searching then for something else.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou understanding?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have,” you will say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For hadst thou not memory, thou couldest not retain what I said; and hadst thou not understanding, thou couldest not comprehend what thou hast retained.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast then this as well as the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou recallest thine understanding unto that which thou dost retain within, and so thou seest it, and by seeing art fashioned into that state as to be said to know.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I am searching for a third thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Memory thou hast, whereby to retain what is said; and understanding thou hast, whereby to understand what is retained; but as touching these two, I ask again of thee, Hast thou not with thy will retained and understood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Undoubtedly, with my will, you will say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou hast will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are the three things which I promised I would bring home to your ears and minds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These three things are in thee, which thou canst number, but canst not separate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These three then, memory, understanding, and will—these three, I say, consider how they are separately exhibited, yet is their operation inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord will be my present help, and I see that He is present to help me; by your understanding what I say, I see that He is present to help me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I perceive by these your voices how that you have understood me, and I surely trust that He will still assist us, that you may comprehend the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I promised to show you three things which are separately exhibited whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then; I did not know what was in thy mind, and thou showedest me by saying, “Memory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This word, this sound, this expression came forth from thy mind to mine ears.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For before that, thou hadst the silent idea of this memory, but thou didst not express it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was in thee, but it had not yet come to me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that that which was in thee might be passed on to me, thou didst express the very word, that is, “Memory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I heard it, I heard these three syllables in the word, “Memory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a noun, a word of three syllables, it sounded, and came to my ear, and impressed a certain idea on my mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sound has passed away, but the word whereby the idea was conveyed, and the idea itself, remains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I ask, when thou didst pronounce this word, “Memory,” thou seest certainly that it has reference to the memory only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the other two things have their own proper names.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For one is called “the understanding,” and the other, “the will,” not the “memory,” but that one alone is called “memory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, whereby didst thou work in order to express this, in order to produce these three syllables?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This word which has reference to the memory only, both memory was engaged in producing in thee, that thou mightest retain what thou saidst, and understanding, that thou mightest know what thou retainedst, and will, that thou mightest give expression to what thou knewest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thanks be to the Lord our God!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath helped us, both you and me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I tell you the truth, beloved, that I undertook the examination and explanation of this subject with exceeding fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I was afraid lest haply I might gladden the spirit of the more enlarged in mind, and inflict on the slower capacities an afflictive weariness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now I see both by the attention with which you have heard, and the quickness with which you have understood me, that you have not only caught what I have said, but that you have anticipated my words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thanks be to the Lord!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, henceforth I speak in all security of that which you have already understood; I am inculcating no unknown lesson, but am only conveying to you by recapitulation what you have already received.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now, of these three things, one only has been yet named and expressed; “Memory” is the name of one only of those three, yet all the three concurred in producing the name of this single one of the three.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The single word “memory” could not be expressed, but by the operation of the will, and the understanding, and the memory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The single word “understanding” could not be expressed, but by the operation of the memory, the will, and the understanding; and the single word “will” could not be expressed, but by the operation of the memory and the understanding and the will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I promised, then, I think has been explained, that which I have pronounced separately, I conceived inseparably.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The three together have produced each one of these, but yet this one which the three have produced has reference not to the three, but to one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The three together have produced the word “memory,” but this word has reference to none but the memory only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The three together have produced the word “understanding,” but it has reference to none but the understanding only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The three together have produced the word “will,” but it has reference to none but the will only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So the Trinity concurred in the formation of the Body of Christ, but it belongs to none but Christ only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Trinity concurred in the formation of the Dove from heaven; but it belongs to none but the Holy Spirit only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Trinity formed the Voice from heaven, but this Voice belongs to none but the Father only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then say to me, no one with unfair cavils try to press upon my infirmity, saying, “Which then of these three, which you have shown to be in our mind or soul, which of them answers to the Father, that is, so to say, to the likeness of the Father, which of them to that of the Son, and which of them to that of the Holy Ghost?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I cannot say—I cannot explain this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us leave somewhat to meditation and to silence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Enter into thine own self; separate thyself from all tumult.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look into thine inner self; see if thou have there some sweet retiring place of conscience, where there may be no noise, no disputation, no strife, or debatings; where there will be not a thought of dissensions, and obstinate contention.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be meek to hear the word, that so thou mayest understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps thou mayest soon have to say, “Thou wilt make me hear of joy and gladness, and my bones shall rejoice;” the bones, that is, which are humbled, not those that are lifted up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is enough, then, that I have shown that there are some three things which are exhibited separately, whose operation is yet inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hast discovered this in thine own self; if thou hast discovered it in man; if thou hast discovered it in a being that walketh on the earth, and beareth about a frail “body, which weigheth down the soul;” believe that the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit may be exhibited separately, by certain visible symbols, by certain forms borrowed from the creatures, and still their operation be inseparable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is enough.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not say that “memory” is the Father,—the “understanding” the Son,—and “will” the Spirit; I do not say this; let men understand it how they will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not venture to say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us reserve the greater truths for those who are capable of them: but, infirm as I am myself, I convey to the infirm only what is according to our powers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not say that these things are in any sort to be equalled with the Holy Trinity, to be squared after an analogy; that is, a kind of exact rule of comparison.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This I do not say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what do I say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have discovered in thee three things, which are exhibited separately, whose operation is inseparable; and of these three, every single name is produced by the three together; yet does not this name belong to the three, but to some one of the three.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe then in the Trinity, what thou canst not see, if in thyself thou hast heard, and seen, and retained it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is in thine own self thou canst know: but what is in Him who made thee, whatever it be, how canst thou know?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if thou shalt be ever able, thou art not able yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And even when thou shalt be able, wilt thou be able so to know God, as He knoweth Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then this suffice you, beloved: I have said all I could; I have made good my promise as ye required.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As to the rest which must be added, that your understanding may make advancement, this seek from the Lord.
